input.background: We wish to estimate the mean weight of a population in which body weight has a log-Normal distribution with $\sigma=40$ pounds. Three independent SRS each of $n=712$ are selected from the population.
input.question: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the third sample with $\bar{x}_3=184$.

output.answer: For sample 3, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_3 \pm m=184 \pm 3=181$ to 187 (pounds).

sigma.description: The standard deviation of the log-Normal distribution of body weight.

n.description: The sample size for each of the three independent simple random samples (SRS).

x_bar_3.description: The sample mean of the third sample.

mu.description: The population mean weight we wish to estimate.

meta_info.caption: 95 % confidence intervals for $\mu, \sigma$ known (Body weight, 20-29-year-old males).
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\\mu$ When $\\sigma$ Is Known
